title: Soy wax massage candle
subtitle: A skin-safe container candle that pools as a warm massage oil
excerpt: A skin-safe soy wax candle with jojoba oil and sweet almond oil that melts to a warm massage oil pool when lit. The blend is 80% soy wax and 20% carrier oils. Burns approximately 20-25 hours and the warm pool can be applied directly to skin.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry massage candle guide (brambleberry.com, accessed May 2026): oil-to-wax ratio for skin-safe melt point and pour consistency. CandleScience notes on soy massage blend formulation (candlescience.com, accessed May 2026).

content.text: A massage candle works by lowering the

of the wax blend so the pool forms at a safe skin temperature (around 40-45°C rather than the 50°C of pure soy wax). This is achieved by blending soy wax with liquid carrier oils at 80:20. The pool can be poured directly onto skin from the edge of the jar, or applied with the fingers. Lavender essential oil at 3% of total weight gives a calming scent and is safe at standard massage dilution levels.
Always test the pool on a small area of skin before wider use. The
is slightly lower than a standard soy candle because of the carrier oil content. Sweet almond oil contains tree nut proteins; label clearly if gifting to anyone with nut sensitivities.
Melt over a simmering-water bath, not direct heat. Never leave melting wax unattended.

items.name: Soy wax (container blend)
items.prepNote: 80% of the wax-oil blend weight. Container-grade soy.

items.name: Jojoba oil
items.prepNote: Approximately 10% of blend weight. Stable, very long shelf life; balances the faster-oxidising sweet almond.

items.name: Sweet almond oil
items.prepNote: Approximately 10% of blend weight. Light emollient. Contains tree nut allergens.

items.name: Lavender essential oil
items.prepNote: 3% of total blend weight (150 g). Safe for skin at massage dilution. Lavandula angustifolia.

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: CD-14 or ECO-10. The carrier oil content reduces the need for a larger wick; use the same wick as for a pure soy jar of the same diameter.

content.text: Fix the wick tab to the centre of the jar base. Thread the wick through the centring bar.

Weigh the soy wax into the candle pouring pitcher and melt over a simmering-water bath to about 60°C. Add the jojoba oil and sweet almond oil, stir to combine, and allow to cool to 55-60°C. Add the lavender essential oil and stir gently for 2 minutes.
Pour the blend into the jar at 55-60°C. The carrier oils make the blend flow more freely than pure wax; pour slowly. Allow to set at room temperature for at least 24 hours before the first use.

Trim the wick to 5 mm and light the candle. The pool forms within 30-45 minutes for a 7 cm jar. Once a pool has formed, tip the jar gently to pour a small amount of warm oil onto the skin, or scoop with a fingertip. Test a small patch of skin before wider use.

Keep in a cool, dark place. The shelf life is 12 months from making, limited by the oxidation timeline of the sweet almond oil. Discard if the pool smells rancid or sharp when the candle is lit. Label clearly with the tree nut content from the sweet almond oil.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 200 ml jar candle (20-25 burn hours, skin-safe melt pool)
